column1: # Lines starting with `#` are comments. EVERY rule below must be
# preceded by a `#` comment giving its one-line reason — enforced by
# the allowlist surfaces it again at WARN; adding one needs a reason.
# 10202 — Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens: NextAuth + Next 16 use a
#   pair) that ZAP can't observe as a form field, so it false-positives
#   on every POST. CSRF is covered; see src/auth.ts.
#   are intentionally cacheable, non-sensitive liveness probes — they
#   must stay reachable + cacheable for monitoring during an attack.
# 10027 — Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments: matches the
#   framework-generated Next.js build manifest / chunk comments, which
#   carry no secrets (no app source comments are shipped to the client).
